purpose: Models the position coder responsible for tool positioning accuracy and spindle orientation control. Tracks signal integrity degradation mechanisms that affect position error, synchronous error, orientation time, and orientation completion status.

inputs.name: orientation_stop_position
inputs.description: Target orientation stop position commanded by the CNC controller, range 0–4095 pulses.

outputs.name: position_error
outputs.description: Difference between commanded and actual coder position; increases as signal integrity degrades.

outputs.name: synchronous_error
outputs.description: Synchronous tracking error between spindle and coder reference; increases with one-rotation signal degradation.

outputs.name: orientation_time
outputs.description: Time elapsed to complete spindle orientation; increases as overall signal integrity degrades.

outputs.name: spindle_sequence_state
outputs.description: Current spindle orientation sequence state (a|b|c|d|e); derived from orientation progress and signal health.

outputs.name: orientation_completed
outputs.description: Boolean flag indicating whether spindle orientation has successfully completed.

states.name: signal_degradation
states.description: Health of the primary position coder signal channel. Starts at 1.0 (perfect) and degrades toward 0.0 as mechanical wear and vibration cause signal dropout or disconnection events. Drives position_error output.

states.name: one_rotation_signal_degradation
states.description: Health of the one-rotation (index pulse) signal channel. Starts at 1.0 and degrades toward 0.0 as detection errors accumulate due to contamination and mechanical wear. Drives synchronous_error output.

states.name: signal_integrity_degradation
states.description: Overall composite signal integrity health of the position coder, capturing noise, wiring fatigue, and encoder disc contamination. Starts at 1.0 and degrades toward 0.0. Drives orientation_time and orientation_completed outputs.

states.name: contamination_level
states.description: Accumulated contamination on the coder disc and optical/magnetic sensing elements from metallic wear particles. Builds from 0.0 (clean) toward 1.0 (fully contaminated). Accelerates signal_integrity_degradation.

states.name: position_error_tracked
states.description: First-order lag tracking state for position error, representing the smoothed deviation between commanded and actual coder position. Approaches the instantaneous position error target with a stability-capped lag coefficient.

events.name: position_coder_disconnected
events.description: Triggered when signal_degradation falls below the critical disconnection threshold, indicating the primary coder signal has been lost due to severe mechanical or electrical degradation.

events.name: one_rotation_signal_detection_error
events.description: Triggered when one_rotation_signal_degradation falls below the detection error warning threshold, indicating intermittent index pulse failures that compromise orientation repeatability.

events.name: one_rotation_signal_not_detected
events.description: Triggered when one_rotation_signal_degradation falls below the critical not-detected threshold, indicating complete loss of the index pulse required for orientation homing.

events.name: position_coder_signal_error
events.description: Triggered when overall signal_integrity_degradation falls below the critical signal error threshold or contamination_level exceeds its critical limit, indicating unrecoverable signal corruption affecting positioning accuracy.

parameters.name: signal_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which the primary position coder signal health degrades per unit of bearing_wear driver per time step. Derived from degradation_timescale; no simulator locked rate matched.

parameters.name: one_rotation_signal_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which the one-rotation index pulse signal health degrades per unit of bearing_wear driver per time step. Derived from degradation_timescale; no simulator locked rate matched.

parameters.name: signal_integrity_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which overall signal integrity health degrades per unit of grease_degradation driver per time step. Matched to grease_degradation_rate from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: contamination_buildup_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which contamination accumulates on the coder disc per time step from metallic wear particles. Matched to contamination_buildup_rate from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_signal_degradation_threshold
parameters.description: signal_degradation level below which position_coder_disconnected (SP0027) event is triggered. Event_state = 1.0 when signal_degradation >= threshold.

parameters.name: one_rotation_warning_threshold
parameters.description: one_rotation_signal_degradation level below which one_rotation_signal_detection_error (SP0041) warning is triggered.

parameters.name: one_rotation_critical_threshold
parameters.description: one_rotation_signal_degradation level below which one_rotation_signal_not_detected (SP0042) failure is triggered.

parameters.name: signal_integrity_critical_threshold
parameters.description: signal_integrity_degradation level below which position_coder_signal_error (SP0047) failure is triggered.

parameters.name: contamination_critical_threshold
parameters.description: contamination_level above which position_coder_signal_error (SP0047) failure is also triggered.

parameters.name: position_error_base_amplitude
parameters.description: Baseline peak position error amplitude in pulses under healthy conditions (signal_degradation = 1.0).

parameters.name: critical_position_error
parameters.description: Critical position error threshold in pulses from simulator ground truth. Used to scale position_error output and event logic.

parameters.name: synchronous_error_base_amplitude
parameters.description: Baseline peak synchronous error amplitude in pulses under healthy one-rotation signal conditions.

parameters.name: orientation_time_nominal
parameters.description: Nominal orientation completion time in milliseconds under fully healthy signal conditions.

parameters.name: orientation_time_max
parameters.description: Maximum orientation time in milliseconds before orientation is considered failed.

parameters.name: position_error_lag_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for position_error_tracked state. Capped at 0.5 for numerical stability.

parameters.name: contamination_signal_coupling
parameters.description: Coupling factor by which contamination_level accelerates signal_integrity_degradation rate.

parameters.name: orientation_completion_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum signal_integrity_degradation level required for orientation_completed to be true.

state_transition_logic: where bearing_wear_driver is the normalized load-cycles-and-speed driver value passed from the bearing subsystem or set to 1.0 if unavailable.
same bearing_wear_driver as above; index pulse is mechanically co-located with the main coder.

signal_integrity_degradation[t+dt] = clamp(signal_integrity_degradation[t] - signal_integrity_degradation_rate * (grease_degradation_driver + contamination_signal_coupling * contamination_level[t]) * dt, 0.0, 1.0)
where grease_degradation_driver is the normalized thermal-stress-and-oxidation driver; contamination_level accelerates degradation via contamination_signal_coupling.
where metallic_wear_driver is the normalized metallic-wear-particles driver from the bearing subsystem.
The instantaneous target for position error grows as signal_degradation falls.
First-order lag approach; position_error_lag_coefficient is capped at 0.5 for stability.
output_logic: The smoothed tracking state directly provides the position error output in pulses.
Under healthy conditions (signal_degradation = 1.0), position_error_target = 0 and position_error remains near 0.
As signal_degradation degrades toward 0.0, position_error grows toward critical_position_error (2000 pulses).
Synchronous error is proportional to the loss of one-rotation signal health.
Healthy: synchronous_error ≈ 0. Fully degraded: synchronous_error ≈ synchronous_error_base_amplitude.
Clamped to [-512, 512] pulses per typical_value range.
Orientation time increases hyperbolically as signal integrity degrades.
Healthy: orientation_time ≈ orientation_time_nominal. Severely degraded: approaches orientation_time_max.
True only when both overall signal integrity and index pulse health are above their critical failure thresholds.
event_logic: event_state = 1.0 when signal_degradation = 1.0 (healthy initial condition satisfied).
event_state = 1.0 when one_rotation_signal_degradation = 1.0 (healthy initial condition satisfied).
event_state = 1.0 when one_rotation_signal_degradation = 1.0 (healthy initial condition satisfied).
event_state = 1.0 when signal_integrity_degradation = 1.0 AND contamination_level = 0.0 (healthy initial condition satisfied).
Event triggers when signal_integrity_degradation <= signal_integrity_critical_threshold (default 0.15) OR contamination_level >= contamination_critical_threshold (default 0.85).
assumptions: signal_degradation_rate (default 0.02) is matched to bearing_wear_rate from simulator ground truth, as the primary coder signal is mechanically coupled to bearing condition. Driver is bearing_wear (load_cycles_and_speed).

one_rotation_signal_degradation_rate (default 0.015) is matched to preload_degradation_rate from simulator ground truth, as the index pulse sensor is sensitive to preload changes affecting disc runout. Driver is bearing_wear (load_cycles_and_speed) as the closest mechanical driver.

signal_integrity_degradation_rate (default 0.03) is matched to grease_degradation_rate from simulator ground truth. Driver is grease_degradation (thermal_stress_and_oxidation), as lubricant breakdown introduces contamination and noise into the optical/magnetic sensing path.
contamination_buildup_rate (default 0.01) is matched to contamination_buildup_rate from simulator ground truth. Driver is contamination_level (metallic_wear_particles).

Event thresholds for critical_signal_degradation_threshold, one_rotation_warning_threshold, one_rotation_critical_threshold, signal_integrity_critical_threshold, and contamination_critical_threshold are not directly provided in the simulator ground truth for this component. Values are set conservatively based on the degradation scale (0.0–1.0) and the pattern of critical_bearing_wear = 0.8 from the ground truth. These should be calibrated against labeled fault data.
critical_position_error = 2000.0 pulses is taken directly from simulator ground truth and used to contextualize the position_error output scaling.

The bearing_wear_driver and metallic_wear_driver are assumed to be normalized scalar inputs (0.0–1.0) passed from the bearing subsystem model. If unavailable, they default to 1.0 (worst-case continuous degradation).
grease_degradation_driver is assumed to be a normalized scalar (0.0–1.0) from the lubrication subsystem. If unavailable, defaults to 1.0.

The spindle_sequence_state output is modeled as a discrete enumeration derived from signal_integrity_degradation thresholds. The exact CNC controller state machine mapping may differ; thresholds should be validated against controller documentation.
position_error_lag_coefficient is set to 0.3 (below the 0.5 stability cap) to provide smooth tracking behavior without oscillation.

The position_error output is modeled as growing with loss of signal_degradation and scaled by orientation_stop_position to reflect that larger commanded positions produce larger absolute errors under degraded conditions.
orientation_time_nominal = 500 ms is an assumed healthy baseline; this should be calibrated from machine commissioning data.
